extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael attacked solo with 200+ meters remaining on uphill finish in Rodez after AVERMAET Greg covered earlier moves, winning by clear daylight as ARU Fabio lost yellow jersey losing 24 seconds.
tactical_patterns: MATTHEWS attacked from 200m+ out on uphill finish after positioning on AVERMAET's wheel, exploiting punchy finale terrain unreachable for pure sprinters.
Team Sunweb collaborated with BMC from race start to control dangerous breakaway, sharing workload allowed MATTHEWS fresher legs despite stage-long chase.
ARU isolated without teammates at back of peloton on descent into valleys before finish, catastrophic positioning cost yellow jersey on non-mountain stage.
KITTEL Marcel dropped on rolling terrain approaching finish, revealing stage profile filtering vulnerability even when not classified as mountain stage.
Sky immediately exploited ARU's weakness to reclaim yellow for FROOME, attacking tempo on approach to demonstrate GC teams monitor non-mountain finishes aggressively.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Won from 200m solo attack after full day's work chasing breakaway, exceptional punch-sprint combination
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Lost yellow jersey and 24 seconds through poor positioning without teammates on descent, catastrophic tactical collapse
rider_signals.AVERMAET Greg: positive: Covered multiple attacks on final climb and nearly caught MATTHEWS, best positioned puncher besides winner
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Reclaimed yellow jersey gaining 24 seconds on ARU through opportunistic positioning on non-mountain stage
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: Dropped on rolling finale terrain with three teammates, green jersey vulnerability exposed outside pure sprints
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Moved into podium position at 1:17 behind FROOME, now team calculus complicated for Sky leadership
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Finished well in uphill sprint, sixth overall showing unexpected GC resilience
